title: cxxrtl: Improve backend debugging output
body:
This is more of an enhancement, but while debugging several cxxrtl issues, it's become apparent that the level of logging and debug output available without code changes makes debugging issues with the backend difficult. 

I'm sure there's many ways to approach this and obviously there's a burden for maintaining such extra code, but my perspective is that it would be nice if at least all the `log_assert` statements were made such that they contained the signal/block being generated at the time. 

This would likely require some thought put in since usually the information desired would be the cell (with its specific  signals) and the name of the module are desired, but the error is caught at a level where only the signal at fault is in scope. But being able to having something like the output of `log_cell/log_wire` be enabled at key points would prevent a lot of compile cycles and allow some preliminary determination of where the issue at fault is. 

A slight issue is that currently things like `log_cell/log_wire` require non-const inputs, but that seems to be solvable since they're not actually mutated.

I have some ideas for where these sites would be given the places I've seen issues crop up recently, but I'd want to think about it be more before proposing anything more than improving places where `log_assert` is fired.
